Case Study

DCE

Proactive data contracts enforcement built from dataprof's profiling core.

Pre-release engine Rust Data Contracts Data Quality Iceberg YAML
Back to workbench ← Nephtys Open Lakehouse Starter →

Why it exists

Profiling is useful after the fact, but data platforms also need an explicit way to define what must be true before pipelines keep moving.

Technical center

DCE grows out of dataprof by moving from observation to enforcement: contract definitions, validation commands, and table-format-aware checks become the center of the workflow.

Current proof points

The shift from profiler to contract engine is already visible in the public repo through Iceberg-aware validation, explicit known limitations for non-Iceberg formats, 194 tests across packages, and a roadmap that makes the next steps in SQL execution and multi-format validation explicit.

Repository
Contract example

Iceberg contract fields and quality rules.

Validation run
